title: Clark the Shark
author: Bruce Hale
description: Clark is a enthusiastic shark who loves school, but he learns that there's a time and a place for everything—and sometimes you have to stay cool.

sentences.englishOriginal: Hey everybody.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 大家好。

sentences.englishOriginal: Welcome to Storyline Online brought to you by the SAG-AFTRA Foundation.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 歡迎來到由 SAG-AFTRA 基金會為您呈現的 Storyline Online。

vocabularyNotes.title: foundation
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 「foundation」指「基金會」或「機構」，通常是非營利性質的組織。

examples.english: The foundation donates money to public schools every year.
examples.chinese: 該基金會每年都會捐款給公立學校。

phraseNotes.title: brought to you by
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用於節目或活動開場，表示「由……贊助/提供/呈現」。

examples.english: Tonight's show is brought to you by our local listeners.
examples.chinese: 今晚的節目是由我們當地的聽眾贊助呈現的。

grammarNotes.title: Past participle phrase modifying a noun
grammarNotes.explanation: 「brought to you by...」是過去分詞片語，修飾前面的專有名詞「Storyline Online」，相當於省略了「which is」。

examples.english: This is a movie directed by a famous director.
examples.chinese: 這是一部由知名導演執導的電影。

sentences.englishOriginal: My name is Chris Pine and today we are reading the wonderful book Clark the Shark written by Bruce Hale and illustrated by Guy Francis.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我是克里斯·潘恩 (Chris Pine)，今天我們要閱讀這本由布魯斯·黑爾 (Bruce Hale) 寫作、蓋伊·弗朗西斯 (Guy Francis) 繪製的精彩圖畫書《克拉克鯊魚》 (Clark the Shark)。

vocabularyNotes.title: illustrate
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「為（書本、文章等）畫插圖」。其過去分詞「illustrated」經常用於介紹繪本插畫家。

examples.english: She has illustrated dozens of popular children's books.
examples.chinese: 她已經為數十本受歡迎的童書畫過插圖。

sentences.englishOriginal: Let us begin.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 讓我們開始吧。

sentences.englishOriginal: In all the wide blue seas, in all the wide blue world, the top school for fish was the Theodore Roosterfish Elementary.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在廣闊蔚藍的大海中，在整個廣大遼闊的世界裡，魚類最棒的學校就是西奧多·公雞魚小學 (Theodore Roosterfish Elementary)。

sentences.englishOriginal: And of all the fish at Theodore Roosterfish, the biggest and the strongest was Clark the Shark.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在西奧多·公雞魚小學的所有魚類中，最大隻、最強壯的就是鯊魚克拉克。

phraseNotes.title: of all the...
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用於最高級句型中，表示「在所有的……之中」。

examples.english: Of all the options available, this one is the most affordable.
examples.chinese: 在所有可用的選項中，這個是最划算的。

sentences.englishOriginal: Clark loved school, and he loved his teacher, Mrs. Inkydink.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 克拉克很喜歡上學，他也非常喜歡他的老師墨水瓶太太 (Mrs. Inkydink)。

sentences.englishOriginal: He loved to play upsy-downsy and spinna-ma-jig with his friends.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他喜歡和朋友們玩「顛顛倒倒」和「轉轉樂」遊戲。

sentences.englishOriginal: Clark loved his life.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 克拉克熱愛他的生活。

sentences.englishOriginal: “SCHOOL IS AWESOME!” shouted Clark the Shark.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「上學太棒了！」鯊魚克拉克大聲喊道。

vocabularyNotes.title: awesome
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，在口語中表示「極好的」、「太棒了」。

examples.english: The movie we watched yesterday was awesome!
examples.chinese: 我們昨天看的電影太棒了！

sentences.englishOriginal: “Less shouting, more reading,” said Mrs. Inkydink.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「少點尖叫，多點閱讀，」墨水瓶太太說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: less X, more Y
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 一種簡潔的命令或建議句型，意思為「少一點X，多一點Y」，常用於糾正行為。

examples.english: Less talking, more working, please.
examples.chinese: 請少說話，多做事。

sentences.englishOriginal: “LUNCHTIME IS SWEEEEET!” yelled Clark the Shark.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「午餐時間太超讚了！」鯊魚克拉克大吼。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: sweet
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 美式俚語，用來讚嘆事物「棒極了」、「太酷了」。

examples.english: You got tickets to the concert? That's sweet!
examples.chinese: 你拿到演唱會門票了？太酷了吧！

sentences.englishOriginal: “Munch your own lunch,” said his best friend, Joey Mackerel.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「吃你自己的午餐就好，」他最好的朋友喬伊·鯖魚 (Joey Mackerel) 說。

vocabularyNotes.title: munch
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「津津有味地大口咀嚼、大聲吃東西」。

examples.english: He sat on the couch munching popcorn.
examples.chinese: 他坐在沙發上津津有味地吃著爆米花。

sentences.englishOriginal: “RECESS ROCKS!” bellowed Clark the Shark.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「下課休息時間太讚了！」鯊魚克拉克吼道。

vocabularyNotes.title: recess
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指學校課間的「下課休息時間」。

examples.english: The children ran outside as soon as recess started.
examples.chinese: 下課時間一到，孩子們就跑到了戶外。

vocabularyNotes.title: bellow
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「吼叫、大聲咆哮」。

examples.english: The coach bellowed instructions from the sideline.
examples.chinese: 教練在場邊大聲喊著指令。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: something rocks
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 口語表達，表示「某事/某物太棒了、超讚」。

examples.english: Your new car really rocks!
examples.chinese: 你的新車真的太酷了！

sentences.englishOriginal: “You are playing too rough, Clark!” cried the other kids.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「克拉克，你玩得太野、太粗魯了！」其他的孩子大喊。

vocabularyNotes.title: rough
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，在這裡指動作「粗魯的、野蠻的」。

examples.english: Don't be so rough with the dog.
examples.chinese: 對那隻狗別那麼粗魯。

sentences.englishOriginal: Yes, Clark loved his life with all of his sharky heart.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 是的，克拉克全心全意地熱愛著他的生活。

phraseNotes.title: with all of one's heart
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「全心全意地」、「真心實意地」。

examples.english: She loved her baby with all of her heart.
examples.chinese: 她全心全意地愛著她的寶寶。

sentences.englishOriginal: But he loved everything way too much.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但他對每件事物的熱情都實在太過頭了。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: way too much
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 美式口語強調法，「way」在這裡作為副詞，用來加強「too much」，表示「實在太……」、「過度」。

examples.english: You spend way too much time playing games.
examples.chinese: 你花在打遊戲上的時間實在太多了。

sentences.englishOriginal: He was too loud.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他太吵了。

sentences.englishOriginal: He was too wild.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他太野了。

sentences.englishOriginal: He was just too much shark for the other fish to handle.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 對其他小魚來說，他的鯊魚能量實在是讓人吃不消。

grammarNotes.title: too... to...
grammarNotes.explanation: 「too + 形容詞/名詞 + for sb to + 動詞」結構，表示「太……以致於某人無法……」。

examples.english: The box is too heavy for me to carry.
examples.chinese: 這個箱子太重了，我搬不動。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: too much to handle
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 常用道地表達，指某人事物「令人難以應付、吃不消」。

examples.english: The noise in the crowded room was too much to handle.
examples.chinese: 擁擠房間裡的噪音實在讓人吃不消。

sentences.englishOriginal: After a while, no one would play with Clark.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 過了一陣子，沒有人願意跟克拉克玩了。

phraseNotes.title: after a while
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「過了一段時間後」、「不久之後」。

examples.english: After a while, the rain stopped and the sun came out.
examples.chinese: 過了一會兒，雨停了，太陽露臉了。

grammarNotes.title: would (past refusal)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「would not / no one would」在這裡表示過去的意願，即「過去不肯、不願意做某事」。

examples.english: The door was stuck and wouldn't open.
examples.chinese: 門卡住了，怎麼都不肯打開。

sentences.englishOriginal: No one ate lunch with Clark.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 沒有人和克拉克一起吃午餐。

sentences.englishOriginal: No one sat with him at circle time.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 團體討論時間也沒有人和他坐在一起。

phraseNotes.title: circle time
phraseNotes.explanation: 幼兒園或小學常見的活動，指師生围坐成一圈進行討論、說故事或活動的「團體時間」。

examples.english: The teacher gathered all the children for circle time.
examples.chinese: 老師把所有的孩子聚集起來進行團體時間。

sentences.englishOriginal: Even his best friend, Joey Mackerel, said, “Cool your jets, Clark! You’re making me crazy!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 連他最好的朋友喬伊·鯖魚都說：「克拉克，冷靜一點！你快把我逼瘋了！」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: cool your jets
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 非常道地的美式俚語，叫人「冷靜下來、冷靜一下」，相當於「calm down」。

examples.english: Cool your jets! We have plenty of time.
examples.chinese: 冷靜點！我們時間還充裕得很。

sentences.englishOriginal: One day, Clark asked Mrs. Inkydink, “What’s wrong with everyone?”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 有一天，克拉克問墨水瓶太太：「大家到底怎麼了？」

phraseNotes.title: what's wrong with...
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用問句，詢問「某人/某事怎麼了、出什麼問題了」。

examples.english: What's wrong with your computer? It keeps restarting.
examples.chinese: 你的電腦怎麼了？它一直重新開機。

sentences.englishOriginal: Mrs. Inkydink patted his fin.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 墨水瓶太太拍了拍他的魚鰭。

vocabularyNotes.title: pat
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指安撫或親暱地「輕拍」。

examples.english: She gently patted the puppy on its head.
examples.chinese: 她輕輕地拍了拍小狗的頭。

vocabularyNotes.title: fin
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指魚類的「鰭」。

examples.english: The shark raised its dorsal fin above the water.
examples.chinese: 鯊魚將它的背鰭升出了水面。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Clark, sometimes you play too hard, you munch too hard, and—gosh—you even help too hard.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「克拉克，有時候你玩得太過頭、吃得太過頭，而且——天哪——你連幫忙都幫得太用力了。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: gosh
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 溫和的感嘆詞，用來表達驚訝、無奈或感歎，相當於「天哪」。

examples.english: Gosh, I didn't realize it was already midnight!
examples.chinese: 天哪，我沒發現居然已經午夜了！

sentences.englishOriginal: “But life is SO exciting!” said Clark.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「但是生活真的太令人興奮了啊！」克拉克說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “There’s a time and a place for everything,” said Mrs. Inkydink.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「凡事都要看時機和場合，」墨水瓶太太說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: There's a time and a place for everything.
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 著名的英文成語/諺語，意思即為「凡事皆有合適的時機與場合」，提醒人行為舉止要合時宜。

examples.english: Stop joking around; there's a time and a place for everything.
examples.chinese: 別再開玩笑了，凡事都要分場合和時機。

sentences.englishOriginal: “And sometimes the rule is stay cool.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「而有時候規則就是：保持冷靜。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: stay cool
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 常用道地表達，指「保持冷靜、冷靜沉著」。

examples.english: Just stay cool and focus on your exam.
examples.chinese: 只要保持冷靜，專心面對考試就好。

sentences.englishOriginal: At recess, Clark tried to stay cool, but he pushed the swing with too much zing!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 下課時間，克拉克努力想保持冷靜，但他推鞦韆時還是推得太猛了！

vocabularyNotes.title: zing
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，口語中指「活力、勁道、衝勁」。

examples.english: Add some lemon juice to give the sauce a bit more zing.
examples.chinese: 加一點檸檬汁可以讓醬汁更有風味和勁道。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Sorry,” said Clark. “I forgot.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「對不起，」克拉克說，「我忘了。」

sentences.englishOriginal: “Yikes!” cried Joey Mackerel.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「哇啊！」喬伊·鯖魚大叫。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: yikes
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 感嘆詞，用來表達輕微的驚嚇、驚恐或驚訝。

examples.english: Yikes! That was a close call.
examples.chinese: 哇啊！剛才好險啊。

sentences.englishOriginal: At lunch, Clark tried to stay cool, but everything smelled so good that he munched a bunch of lunches.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 午餐時間，克拉克試著保持冷靜，但每樣食物聞起來都太香了，結果他大口大口地塞了一堆午餐。

phraseNotes.title: a bunch of
phraseNotes.explanation: 美式口語片語，指「大量的、一堆的」。

examples.english: I bought a bunch of bananas from the store.
examples.chinese: 我從商店買了一大串香蕉。

grammarNotes.title: so... that...
grammarNotes.explanation: 「so + 形容詞 + that 子句」結構，表示「如此……以致於……」。

examples.english: The movie was so funny that everyone laughed.
examples.chinese: 這部電影太有趣了，大家都在笑。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Sorry,” said Clark. “I forgot.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「對不起，」克拉克說，「我忘了。」

sentences.englishOriginal: “We’re STARVING!” said his friends.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我們快要餓死了啦！」他的朋友們說。

vocabularyNotes.title: starve
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，原意為「挨餓」，口語中常用進行式「starving」極度誇張地表達「非常餓」。

examples.english: What's for dinner? I'm starving!
examples.chinese: 晚餐吃什麼？我快餓死了！

sentences.englishOriginal: In class, Clark tried to stay cool, but a good book got him all shook up.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 課堂上，克拉克試著保持冷靜，但一本精彩的好書讓他情緒整個激動起來。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: shook up
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 俚語表達（來自 get shook up），形容「情緒激動、興奮或受到震撼」。

examples.english: The surprising news left everyone completely shook up.
examples.chinese: 這令人震驚的消息讓大家情緒都相當激動。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Now, Clark!” said Mrs. Inkydink. “This isn’t the time or the place.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「克拉克，現在不行喔！」墨水瓶太太說，「現在不是時候，這裡也不是場合。

sentences.englishOriginal: Tell me, what’s the rule?”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 告訴我，規則是什麼？」

sentences.englishOriginal: “Stay cool,” said Clark. “Hey, that rhymes!” he cried.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「保持冷靜，」克拉克說。「嘿，這有押韻耶！」他喊道。

vocabularyNotes.title: rhyme
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「押韻」。

examples.english: The words 'cat' and 'hat' rhyme.
examples.chinese: cat 和 hat 這兩個字押韻。

sentences.englishOriginal: Then Clark got a big idea in his sharky head.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然後，克拉克那顆鯊魚腦袋裡浮現了一個大妙招。

phraseNotes.title: get an idea
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「想到一個主意/好辦法」。

examples.english: She got a brilliant idea for her science project.
examples.chinese: 她為她的科學展覽想到了一個絕妙的主意。

sentences.englishOriginal: Maybe if I make a rhyme, I’ll remember every time! he thought.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 如果我編個順口溜押韻，這樣我每次就都會記得了！他心想。

grammarNotes.title: First Conditional (if + present, will + verb)
grammarNotes.explanation: 第一條件句，表達對未來可能發生情況的假設，結構為「If + 現在式動詞, 將來式 (will) + 原形動詞」。

examples.english: If it rains tomorrow, we will stay inside.
examples.chinese: 如果明天下雨，我們就會待在室內。

sentences.englishOriginal: The next day, he put his plan to work.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 隔天，他開始執行他的計畫。

phraseNotes.title: put something to work
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用片語，表示「將（計畫、技巧、想法等）付諸實行、投入使用」。

examples.english: He put his computer skills to work in his new job.
examples.chinese: 他在新工作中發揮了自己的電腦技能。

sentences.englishOriginal: In class, when lessons got exciting, Clark wanted to bounce up out of his seat.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 上課時，當課程變得很有趣，克拉克又忍不住想要從座位上蹦跳起來。

vocabularyNotes.title: bounce
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「彈跳、蹦跳」。

examples.english: The excited children began to bounce on the bed.
examples.chinese: 興奮的孩子們開始在床上跳來跳去。

sentences.englishOriginal: Instead, he told himself: “When teacher’s talking, don’t go walking.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但這次他告訴自己：「老師說話時，不要亂走動。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: don't go doing something
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 口語中加強口氣的禁止句型，比單純說「don't walk」更具提醒和警戒意味。

examples.english: Don't go touching things in the museum.
examples.chinese: 可別去摸博物館裡的展品喔。

sentences.englishOriginal: And what do you know? It worked!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 你猜怎麼著？這招真的管用！

nativeExpressionNotes.title: what do you know?
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 極為道地的英文口語感嘆句，用來表達「神奇吧、你猜怎麼著、沒想到吧」。

examples.english: I fixed the issue all by myself. What do you know?
examples.chinese: 我自己把問題搞定了，神奇吧？

sentences.englishOriginal: “Attaboy, Clark!” said Mrs. Inkydink. Clark smiled. “Lessons are fun!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「好樣的，克拉克！」墨水瓶太太讚許道。克拉克笑了：「上課真有趣！」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: attaboy
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 美式俚語感嘆詞，用來表揚或鼓勵男孩子或男士，表示「幹得好！好樣的！」。

examples.english: Attaboy! That was a great catch!
examples.chinese: 好樣的！接得太漂亮了！

sentences.englishOriginal: At lunch, everything smelled sooo yummy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 午餐時間，每樣東西聞起來都好——美味。

vocabularyNotes.title: yummy
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，兒童口語或日常俚語，表示「美味的、好吃的」。

examples.english: This chocolate cake is so yummy!
examples.chinese: 這個巧克力蛋糕真好吃！

sentences.englishOriginal: When Clark wanted to eat and eat and never stop, he told himself: “Only munch your own lunch.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當克拉克又想大吃特吃、停不下來時，他提醒自己：「只吃你自己的午餐。」

sentences.englishOriginal: And it worked again! “Way to go, Clark!” said his friends.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這招又成功了！「幹得好，克拉克！」他的朋友們說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: way to go
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 道地的讚賞口語，用來恭喜或稱讚對方「做得好、幹得漂亮」。

examples.english: You passed the exam? Way to go!
examples.chinese: 你通過考試了？幹得漂亮！

sentences.englishOriginal: Clark grinned. “Lunch is fun.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 克拉克咧嘴笑著說：「午餐真有趣。」

vocabularyNotes.title: grin
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「露齒咧嘴笑」。

examples.english: He grinned broadly when he won the award.
examples.chinese: 獲獎時，他高興得咧嘴大笑。

sentences.englishOriginal: At playtime, Clark told himself: “Easy does it, that’s the way. Then my friends will let me play.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 遊戲時間，克拉克告訴自己：「慢慢來，放輕點，這樣才對。這樣朋友們才會跟我玩。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: easy does it
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 常用道地成語/俚語，用來提醒對方「小心點、動作放輕點、慢慢來」。

examples.english: Easy does it! Don't drop the glass vase.
examples.chinese: 小心點！別把玻璃花瓶摔破了。

sentences.englishOriginal: And playtime was fun. Once more, Clark loved his life.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 遊戲時間變得非常愉快。克拉克再次熱愛起了他的生活。

phraseNotes.title: once more
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意為「再次、再一次」。

examples.english: Please explain it once more.
examples.chinese: 請再解釋一次。

sentences.englishOriginal: But then a shadow fell across the playground—a gi-normous shadow with tentacles galore.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但就在這時，一道陰影籠罩了操場——那是一道帶著無數觸手的巨無霸陰影。

vocabularyNotes.title: ginormous
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，由 gigantic 與 enormous 組合而成的口語字，表示「巨大的、龐大的」。

examples.english: Look at that ginormous dog over there!
examples.chinese: 看那邊那隻龐大的狗！

vocabularyNotes.title: tentacle
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指章魚、烏賊等海洋生物的「觸手、觸角」。

examples.english: The octopus wrapped its tentacles around the rock.
examples.chinese: 章魚用它的觸手纏住了岩石。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: galore
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 形容詞（總是放在名詞後面），表示「大量的、豐富的、無數的」。

examples.english: There were games and prizes galore at the fair.
examples.chinese: 園遊會上有許許多多的遊戲和豐富的獎品。

sentences.englishOriginal: “It’s a new kid, and he looks scary!” cried Joey Mackerel. “Swim for your lives!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「是個新來的轉學生，他看起來好可怕！」喬伊·鯖魚大叫。「大家快逃命啊！」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: run / swim for your life
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 常用道地表達，指「拼命逃命、逃奔保命」。

examples.english: When the siren sounded, everyone ran for their lives.
examples.chinese: 警報響起時，大家紛紛拼命逃命。

sentences.englishOriginal: The squid squashed the slide, and it snapped off the swings.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那隻烏賊壓扁了滑梯，還壓斷了鞦韆。

vocabularyNotes.title: squash
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「壓扁、擠壓」。

examples.english: Accidentally, he squashed the tomato in his bag.
examples.chinese: 他不小心把包包裡的番茄壓扁了。

phraseNotes.title: snap off
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，指「硬生生折斷、斷裂」。

examples.english: The strong wind caused the tree branch to snap off.
examples.chinese: 強風導致樹枝折斷了。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Oops. My bad,” said the new kid.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「噢，抱歉，是我的錯，」新來的轉學生說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: my bad
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 非常常用的口語俚語，簡短表示「是我的錯、抱歉」。

examples.english: Sorry I spilled the water, my bad!
examples.chinese: 抱歉我把水打翻了，是我的錯！

sentences.englishOriginal: “Wait,” said Clark. “He just wants to play. Let’s find a way!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「等等，」克拉克說，「他只是想玩而已。我們來想個辦法吧！」

sentences.englishOriginal: And he swam at the new kid with all his might.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 於是，他使出全身的勁道朝新來的轉學生游去。

vocabularyNotes.title: might
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「力量、威力、勁道」。

examples.english: He pushed the heavy door with all his might.
examples.chinese: 他使出全身的力氣推開那扇重門。

phraseNotes.title: with all one's might
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「使盡全力地、拼命地」。

examples.english: She pulled the rope with all her might.
examples.chinese: 她使盡全力拉著繩子。

sentences.englishOriginal: Clark played harder than he ever had before—upsy-downsy and spinna-ma-jig.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 克拉克比以往任何時候都更加賣力地玩著——玩了「顛顛倒倒」和「轉轉樂」。

grammarNotes.title: Comparative with Past Perfect
grammarNotes.explanation: 「harder than he ever had before」是比較級配合過去完成式，強調「比過去任何時候都更……」。

examples.english: He worked harder than he ever had before to pass the test.
examples.chinese: 為了通過測試，他比以往任何時候都更加努力工作。

sentences.englishOriginal: Why, he even made up a new game: tail-whump-a-lumpus!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 哇，他甚至還發明了一個新遊戲：甩尾大碰撞！

phraseNotes.title: make up
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，在這裡指「捏造、編造或發明（遊戲、故事等）」。

examples.english: The kids loved to make up their own games in the yard.
examples.chinese: 孩子們喜歡在院子裡發明自己的遊戲。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Why, ...
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 當「Why」放在句首作為感嘆詞時，並不表示「為什麼」，而是用來表達驚訝、恍然大悟或強調口氣。

examples.english: Why, I never expected to see you here!
examples.chinese: 哎呀，我真沒想到會在這裡遇見你！

sentences.englishOriginal: “Wow, that was fun,” said the new kid breathlessly, and he settled down.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「哇，太好玩了，」新來的轉學生上氣不接下氣地說，然後平靜了下來。

vocabularyNotes.title: breathlessly
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 副詞，指「上氣不接下氣地、喘氣地」。

examples.english: He arrived breathlessly after running up the stairs.
examples.chinese: 他跑上樓梯後，上氣不接下氣地到了。

phraseNotes.title: settle down
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「安頓下來、冷靜/平靜下來」。

examples.english: The students quickly settled down when the teacher entered.
examples.chinese: 老師進來時，學生們很快就安靜了下來。

sentences.englishOriginal: “If you want to come to school, you’ve got to stay cool,” said Clark.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「如果你想要來上學，你就得保持冷靜，」克拉克說。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: have got to
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 極為常見的日常口語表達，表示「必須、不得不」，相當於「must」或「have to」。

examples.english: You've got to try this delicious dessert!
examples.chinese: 你一定要嚐嚐這個美味的甜點！

sentences.englishOriginal: “That’s right, Clark,” said Mrs. Inkydink.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「說得對，克拉克，」墨水瓶太太說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “And thanks for taking care of our new classmate, Sid the Squid.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「還有，謝謝你照顧我們新來的同學，烏賊西德 (Sid the Squid)。」

phraseNotes.title: take care of
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「照顧、關照」。

examples.english: Thanks for taking care of my cat while I was away.
examples.chinese: 謝謝你在我不在的時候照顧我的貓。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Hooray for Clark the Shark!” everyone cheered.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「為鯊魚克拉克歡呼！」大家高聲喊道。

phraseNotes.title: hooray for...
phraseNotes.explanation: 用來為某人或某事喝彩歡呼的表達語，意為「為……萬歲/歡呼」。

examples.english: Hooray for the winning team!
examples.chinese: 為獲勝的隊伍歡呼！

sentences.englishOriginal: That night Clark’s mother asked, “What did you learn at school, dear?”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 那天晚上，克拉克的媽媽問道：「親愛的，你今天在學校學到了什麼？」

sentences.englishOriginal: “There’s a time and a place for everything,” Clark said.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「凡事都要看時機和場合，」克拉克說。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Sometimes you stay cool.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「有時候你要保持冷靜。」

sentences.englishOriginal: “But sometimes a shark’s gotta do what a shark’s gotta do.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「但有時候，一隻鯊魚該出手時還是得出手。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: A man's gotta do what a man's gotta do.
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 改編自著名的經典美式俚語/俗語，意思是「該做的事還是要做」、「責任所在，不得不為」。

examples.english: I don't like getting up early, but a worker's gotta do what a worker's gotta do.
examples.chinese: 我不喜歡早起，但打工人該做的事還是得做。

sentences.englishOriginal: The end.
sentences.chineseTranslation: （故事）結束。

sentences.englishOriginal: That was Clark the Shark everybody.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 各位，以上就是《克拉克鯊魚》的故事。

sentences.englishOriginal: I think it speaks to all of us.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我覺得這故事引起了我們所有人的共鳴。

phraseNotes.title: speak to someone
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，在這裡指「引起某人的共鳴、觸動某人」。

examples.english: The poem really speaks to people who are feeling lonely.
examples.chinese: 這首詩真的觸動了那些感到孤獨的人。

sentences.englishOriginal: It talks about how we all want to do what we want to do, right?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 它講述了我們其實都想做自己想做的事，對吧？

sentences.englishOriginal: We want to be ourselves.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們想要做自己。

sentences.englishOriginal: And we want to yell when we want to yell and play and laugh and have a good time.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當我們想大喊時就想大喊，想玩耍、想笑、想過得開心。

phraseNotes.title: have a good time
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用片語，表示「玩得開心、過得愉快」。

examples.english: We had a great time at the party last night.
examples.chinese: 我們昨晚在派對上玩得很開心。

sentences.englishOriginal: But as Mrs. Inkydink said, there's a time and a place for everything.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但正如墨水瓶太太所說的，凡事都要看時機和場合。

sentences.englishOriginal: And there is a time and a place to be considerate of other people's feelings.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而且，我們也需要有體諒他人感受的時機與場合。

vocabularyNotes.title: considerate
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，指「體貼的、體諒的、體恤的」。

examples.english: It was very considerate of you to offer your seat.
examples.chinese: 你主動讓座真是太體貼了。

phraseNotes.title: be considerate of
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指「體諒……、考慮到……」。

examples.english: Please be considerate of your neighbors at night.
examples.chinese: 夜間請體諒你的鄰居。

sentences.englishOriginal: I think the lesson is: Be considerate.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我覺得這個故事給我們的啟示是：要懂得體諒他人。

sentences.englishOriginal: Thank you so much for watching Storyline Online.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 非常感謝大家收看 Storyline Online。

sentences.englishOriginal: Make sure to check out all of the other stories.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 一定要去看看其他的精彩故事喔。

phraseNotes.title: make sure to
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意為「務必、一定要做某事」。

examples.english: Make sure to lock the door before you leave.
examples.chinese: 離開前務必鎖門。

phraseNotes.title: check out
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，口語中指「去看看、欣賞、查看」。

examples.english: You should check out that new restaurant downtown.
examples.chinese: 你應該去看看市中心那家新開的餐廳。

sentences.englishOriginal: Keep watching and keep reading.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 保持收看，保持閱讀。

phraseNotes.title: keep doing something
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，表示「持續做某事、保持某種狀態」。

examples.english: Keep practicing and you will get better.
examples.chinese: 保持練習，你就會越來越進步。
